title: RIOT Kentucky Facility Expansion and 2024-2025 Guidance Revisions
text: RIOT Platforms acquired Block Mining Inc. in July 2024, establishing an additional arm of growth in Kentucky with 60 megawatts of existing operational capacity and potential to expand to over 300 megawatts in total capacity. However, expansion timelines have faced delays that impacted the company's earnings guidance and hash rate deployment schedules. The Kentucky facilities, which had been anticipated to come online in 2024, were pushed to 2025, and additional expansion plans in Kentucky that were initially planned for 2025 have been deferred into 2026. These delays were cited in Q3 2024 guidance revisions, where the company reduced 2024 deployed hash rate guidance from 36.3 exahash to 34.9 exahash, and 2025 guidance from 56.6 exahash to 46.7 exahash. The extended equipment lead times and slower than planned facility expansion at Kentucky were the primary reasons cited for guidance reductions. The Kentucky facilities remain part of RIOT's long-term capacity expansion strategy, with the company targeting approximately 22% hash rate growth in 2025 despite the Kentucky delays. The facility represents an important geographic diversification of RIOT's mining operations beyond its primary Texas footprint at Corsicana and Rockdale, reducing operational and regulatory risk concentration.
